Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Standard Hire to Retire Process The normative sequence typically follows 1 Post Job Advertisement 2 Screen Candidates 3 Conduct Interviews 4 Make Hiring Decision 5 Onboard Employee 6 Add to Payroll 7 Close Case This is a linear sequence where each step depends on the previous one

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Looking at the edges Post Screen Screen Decide Screen Interview Decide Onboard Onboard Payroll Payroll Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Actually the order says Screen Interview and Screen Decide but there is no edge from Interview to Decide This means Interview and Decide can happen in any order relative to each other after Screen

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In a normative process you would conduct interviews BEFORE making a hiring decision So you could have Decide happening before Interview completes which is a major logical issue

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Actually looking more carefully in a standard process the order would be Screen Interview Decide The model has Screen Decide and Screen Interview but no order between Decide and Interview which means Decide could happen before Interview This is a significant anomaly

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Looking at the edges Post Screen Post Interview Interview Decide Decide loop onboarding loop onboarding xor payroll xor payroll Close

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The structure loop onboarding equals Onboard loop with skip So it is like doing Onboard and possibly doing it again xor payroll equals Payroll or skip

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Interview can happen before Screen since there is no edge Screen Interview and Post Interview exists This means you could conduct interviews without screening candidates first which is unusual

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The loop allows Onboard to be executed multiple times This is unusual you typically onboard an employee once This is an anomaly

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The payroll step can be skipped Skipping payroll is a severe anomaly because adding an employee to payroll is a critical part of hiring

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait let me think about this again

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Actually looking at Model 2 more carefully Post Screen and Post Interview Screen and Interview are not ordered relative to each other Interview Decide So Decide must come after Interview but not necessarily after Screen

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: The bigger issues in Model 2 are The loop on Onboarding allows repeated onboarding which is odd The XOR on Payroll allows skipping payroll entirely which is a major issue

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 has one significant anomaly the hiring decision can be made before during interviews which is a logical issue but the rest of the process is intact Model 2 has multiple anomalies

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 1 is closer to the normative process because while it has a concurrency issue between Decide and Interview all the necessary steps are guaranteed to occur Model 2 allows the process to complete without adding the employee to payroll which is a critical omission

Name: Validation - PE
Text: In Model 1 the activity Conduct Interviews and Make Hiring Decision both still need to happen In Model 2 the XOR on Payroll means Payroll may not happen at all This is a more severe anomaly because the process can complete without a critical activity

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So my conclusion is that Model 1 is more aligned with the normative Hire to Retire process
